Director of Payroll - Global
source_jobs.description: <p style="text-align: left;">Director of Payroll - Global<br><br><br><strong>Role Summary:&nbsp;</strong><br><br>The Director of Payroll – Global Shared Services provides strategic leadership and operational oversight for multi-country payroll delivery within a centralized, global shared services model. This role is responsible for driving consistent, compliant, and scalable payroll operations across regions, while ensuring strong governance, internal controls, and financial rigor in a public company / SOX-regulated environment. The role serves as a key partner to Finance, HR, Internal Audit, and Technology and acts as a global payroll subject-matter expert, with deep experience leveraging ADP, CloudPay, and Workday, and owning payroll accruals, controls, and audit readiness at scale.&nbsp;<br><br><strong>Key Responsibilities:<br><br>Global Payroll Oversight &amp; Shared Services Leadership<br><br></strong>• Provide strategic oversight for end-to-end global payroll operations delivered through a shared services model. <br>• Lead and develop global payroll teams supporting multiple regions and countries. <br>• Serve as the primary global escalation point for complex payroll issues, controls, and risk events. <br>• Drive alignment between in-country payroll execution and global payroll standards.<br><br><strong>Governance, Risk &amp; SOX Controls</strong> <br>• Own and maintain global payroll governance frameworks, including SOPs, segregation of duties, and approval workflows. <br>• Ensure payroll processes comply with SOX and internal control requirements. <br>• Partner with Internal Audit and External Audit to support walkthroughs, testing, and remediation. <br>• Identify and mitigate payroll-related risks across regions, vendors, and systems. <br><br><strong>Financial Management, Accruals &amp; Close</strong> <br>• Own global payroll accrual processes including wages, bonuses, commissions, leave, severance, and statutory costs. <br>• Partner with Finance on month-end and year-end close, GL postings, and reconciliations. <br>• Ensure payroll data is audit-ready and supports public company reporting standards. <br><br><strong>Payroll Systems &amp; Technology Enablement</strong> <br>• Provide leadership and oversight for ADP, CloudPay, and Workday payroll ecosystems. <br>• Partner with HRIS, Finance Systems, and IT to optimize integrations and controls. <br>• Support global payroll implementations, vendor transitions, and system upgrades. &nbsp;<br><br><strong>Vendor &amp; Stakeholder Management</strong> <br>• Manage relationships with global payroll vendors and advisors. <br>• Monitor SLAs and performance metrics. <br>• Act as a trusted partner to HR, Total Rewards, Finance, and Audit stakeholders. <br><br><strong>Process Excellence &amp; Continuous Improvement</strong> <br>• Drive standardization and scalability across payroll operations. <br>• Identify automation and risk-reduction opportunities. <br>• Support long-term payroll transformation initiatives.<br><br><strong>Required Qualifications</strong> <br>• 12+ years of progressive payroll experience with global and/or shared services leadership. <br>• Experience operating in a public company or SOX-controlled environment. <br>• Hands-on experience with ADP, CloudPay, and Workday. <br>• Deep expertise in payroll accruals, reconciliations, and financial close. <br>• Experience managing multi-country payroll operations. <br>• Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.<br><br><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong>

notes: Zeta Global is a marketing cloud company with a strong focus on AI and data analytics. The company has a large number of job postings in sales, marketing, and customer success, indicating a sales-heavy primary function. However, there are also some technical roles, such as engineering and data science positions, which suggests a moderate tech role ratio.
